1. `pytest tests/unit/gateways/github/metadata_blocks/test_state_sparkline.py -v` 2. `pytest tests/unit/cli/commands/objective/ -v --no-header -q` 3. `pytest tests/tui/test_plan_table.py tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py tests/tui/screens/test_objective_nodes_screen.py -v --no-header -q` 4. `pytest tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_dash_data.py -v --no-header -q`
Report pass/fail counts and any failures with full error messages.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run TUI tests (plan table, execute command, objective nodes screen)

============================= test session starts ============================== platform linux -- Python 3.13.12, pytest-9.0.2, pluggy-1.6.0 rootdir: /home/runner/work/erk/erk configfile: pyproject.toml plugins: anyio-4.12.1, cov-7.0.0, xdist-3.8.0, asyncio-1.3.0 asyncio: mode=Mode.AUTO, debug=False, asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope=function, asyncio_default_test_loop_scope=function collected 14 items

============================== 14 passed in 0.06s ==============================

Exit code 1 ......................................................F................. [ 80%] ................. [100%] =================================== FAILURES =================================== __ TestExecuteCommandCopyCommands.test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command __
self = <tests.tui.commands.test_execute_command.TestExecuteCommandCopyCommands object at 0x7ff4e6e22190>
def test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command(self) -> None: """copy_pr_checkout_script copies the PR checkout command with --script.""" row = make_pr_row(123, "Test") executor = FakeCommandExecutor() screen = PlanDetailScreen(row=row, executor=executor, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS) screen.execute_command("copy_pr_checkout_script") expected_cmd = 'source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"' > assert executor.copied_texts == [expected_cmd] E assert ['source <(er...23 --script)'] == ['source "$(e...3 --script)"'] E E At index 0 diff: 'source <(erk pr checkout 123 --script)' != 'source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"' E E Full diff: E [ E - 'source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"', E ? ^^ - E + 'source <(erk pr checkout 123 --script)', E ? ^ E ]
tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py:107: AssertionError =========================== short test summary info ============================
... [12 lines omitted] ...
FAILED tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py::TestExecuteCommandCopyCommands::test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command - assert ['source <(er...23 --script)'] == ['source "$(e...3 --script)"'] 1 failed, 88 passed in 0.46s
session.assistant:
## Test Results Summary
### Test 1: `test_state_sparkline.py` **PASSED: 14 passed in 0.06s**
### Test 2: `tests/unit/cli/commands/objective/` **PASSED: 81 passed in 1.34s**
### Test 3: TUI tests (plan_table, execute_command, objective_nodes_screen) **FAILED: 1 failed, 88 passed in 0.46s**
**Failure Details:** - **Test:** `TestExecuteCommandCopyCommands.test_copy_pr_checkout_script_copies_command` - **File:** `tests/tui/commands/test_execute_command.py:107` - **Issue:** Expected command format mismatch - Expected: `'source "$(erk pr checkout 123 --script)"'` - Actual: `'source <(erk pr checkout 123 --script)'`
The implementation is using process substitution syntax `<(...)` instead of command substitution with double quotes `"$(...)"`.
### Test 4: `test_dash_data.py` **PASSED: 6 passed in 0.07s**
---
**Summary:** - Total: 4 test files - Passed: 3 files (101 tests) - Failed: 1 file (1 test failure out of 89 tests) - Overall: 189 passed, 1 failed
